JOB TITLE: Senior Product Manager, Kits LOCATION: US REMOTE THE ROLE: We're looking for a senior, highly autonomous Product Manager to help grow and develop the Kits AI product line and business. Kits is Splice's AI vocal creation product: a tool that lets producers generate, transform, and create studio quality vocals with ease. This is a rare opportunity: real ownership of growth and performance for a growing product, paired with the freedom to shape and launch entirely new product experiences alongside it. You'll identify high-impact opportunities, productize new ML capabilities into creator-facing workflows, and drive impact from day one. You'll operate across both growth and product development (funnel optimization, pricing, monetization, and retention on one side; product strategy and new launches on the other), and you'll build a deep understanding of customer needs through research, analytics, and internal insight systems. Throughout, you'll use the latest AI tools to meaningfully increase your speed and leverage. WHAT YOU’LL DO: Own product growth and performance for Kits AI across funnel optimization, activation, retention, pricing, and monetization. Drive Kits product strategy: identify high-impact opportunities and launch product and growth initiatives that move the business. Productize new ML capabilities into valuable creator-facing products and workflows, partnering closely with research and engineering. Develop a deep customer understanding through user interviews, analytics, and automated customer insight pipelines. Track product performance in our data-analysis platform, own experimentation, and use data to make sharp prioritization calls. Leverage the latest AI tools to dramatically accelerate your workflow, from shipping features directly to production with AI coding tools to building custom internal systems for customer insight, research, and product discovery. JOB REQUIREMENTS: 2–5 years of product management experience in a fast-moving, consumer or creator-facing environment, ideally a highly autonomous startup environment. A track record of driving growth, product quality, or business performance through product work. Comfort operating across strategy, execution, analytics, and customer research. A metrics-informed mindset: fluent in funnel analysis, product analytics, and event tracking platforms like Amplitude. Experience working on AI- or ML-powered products, or closely with research and engineering teams. Strong judgment, high agency, and the ability to go from ambiguous opportunity to shipped product without waiting to be told. Enthusiasm for using the latest AI tools to increase speed, leverage, and execution quality. NICE TO HAVES: Experience in music, creator tools, or the broader creative industry. Familiarity with growth funnels, pricing and monetization experiments, or lifecycle engagement. Exposure to AI-assisted workflows for research synthesis, spec writing, or internal tooling. SPLICE BENEFITS: Compensation & Equity: Competitive pay with annual reviews and equity opportunities. Time Off: Unlimited PTO to recharge and thrive. Health & Wellness: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents. Retirement Savings: 401(k) plan with immediate vesting and company match. Parental Support: 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave for non-birthing parents, and 18-20 weeks for birthing parents. Flexible Work Opti

jobs.title: Product Manager, ML Research
jobs.description: Product Jobs / Product Manager, ML Research Posted 5 days ago Suno Product Manager, ML Research 5 day week Unlimited PTO Remote · USA Job Description About Suno About Suno We're building the world's first creative entertainment platform, where the entire world can feel the joy and fulfilment of making music. Music is for everyone: Our users include everyone from grandmothers creating songs for their loved ones, to Grammy winners using Suno Studio, our power tool, to make the most popular hits in the world. Building the future of entertainment requires ambition. The pace is fast, the problems are hard, and the work demands ownership and intensity. For the right people, it’s incredibly rewarding: a chance to shape a new medium, work with a small team that cares deeply about quality, make music, drink too much coffee, and build something that millions of people use to express themselves in ways that were never before possible. Suno is the fastest growing consumer entertainment company and the leader in AI music. We are backed by leading investors including Bond Capital, Menlo Ventures, Lightspeed Venture Partners, IVP, Forerunner, Union Square Ventures, Alkeon, Quiet, Matrix Partners, Schroders Capital and, NVentures (venture arm of NVIDIA). Founded in 2023 by Harvard alumni with a shared passion for music, Suno has quickly grown to 200 employees while empowering more than 100 million people to create original songs. In 2026, we raised a $400 million Series D at a $5.4 billion valuation to continue building innovative products that amplify creativity and imagination. About the Role Artificial intelligence is transforming every creative field. At Suno, we are building one of the world's leading generative music models, enabling millions of people to become musicians for the first time. We are hiring the first Product Manager dedicated to our Research organization. In this role, you will partner closely with world-class machine learning engineers to identify the highest-impact opportunities for improving our models. By combining user understanding with rigorous evaluation and product judgment, you will help researchers focus on the problems whose solutions have the greatest impact on our business. This is a rare opportunity to bring product judgment into the model development process: helping ensure our research efforts are grounded in real user needs, evaluated thoughtfully, and translated into meaningful improvements in what people can create with Suno. Listen to the song we made about it: https://suno.com/s/Y0WfEP7qALvaZanH What You'll Do Define what “good” means — Own the evaluation frameworks and quality metrics for Suno’s foundational music model: what we measure, why it matters, and how we know the model is improving. Connect creator needs to model capabilities — Deeply understand how people create with Suno, identify where the model helps or falls short, and translate those insights into research problems the team can act on. Bridge product and research — Partner with user-facing PMs, engineers, and data scientists to understand real-world model behavior and turn those learnings into better evaluations, clearer priorities, and more useful capabilities. Shape model priorities in partnership with Research — Bring user insight, data, and business context into roadmap discussions, helping the team make thoughtful tradeoffs when research opportunities and product needs compete. Bring new models and capabilities to market — Work with Research, Product, and Product Marketing to launch model improvements in a way users can understand, adopt, and get value from. Go deep technically — Run experiments, dig into model behavior, and work in the details alongside researchers and data scientists. Who You Are A product leader with a deep passion and curiosity for AI: 6+ years of product management experience, or equivalent experience building highly technical products Experience partnering closely with machine learning researchers, model training teams, or AI evaluation systems Strong technical intuition and the ability to engage deeply with complex machine learning concepts A strong technical foundation, ideally with a background in computer science, engineering, mathematics, physics, or a related field Exceptional product judgment and the ability to bring clarity and structure to ambiguous, research-driven problems A first-principles thinker who is naturally curious about how frontier AI models work and how they can be improved Operates with urgency and thrives in fast-moving, iterative environments Excited by the intersection of AI, creativity, and music Bonus Points Experience working at a frontier AI lab or on a foundation model team Experience designing or building AI evaluation frameworks, benchmarks, or human evaluation systems Experience working with large-scale datasets, data quality, or annotation pipeline. Additional Notes Applicants must be eligible to work in the US. This role is expected to work from the

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
jobs.description: Product Jobs / Principal Product Manager Posted 6 days ago Seeq Principal Product Manager 5 day week Unlimited PTO Remote · USA Job Description About Seeq The industrial sector generates more data than almost any other, and Seeq is on a mission to help customers use it all to make better decisions. We're the market leader in advanced analytics for process manufacturing, helping the world's largest companies in pharma, energy, chemicals, and more turn massive streams of time-series data into real operational impact. We're approaching $100M ARR, recognized as a Leader by industry analysts, and are accelerating our AI-first product strategy to redefine what Decision Intelligence means for industry. We're looking for a Principal Product Manager who operates as an enterprise-level problem solver: someone who can set a multi-year vision, orchestrate complex cross-functional work, and influence strategy across products, customers, and partners. You’ll own a critical product domain that cuts across teams and capabilities within a platform used by some of the biggest enterprises on the planet. You'll own a significant product area within a platform used by some of the biggest enterprises on the planet, and you'll do it in a culture that values data over opinions, speed over perfection, and impact over activity. This is not a feature factory. You will be expected to make hard tradeoffs, say no as much as you say yes, and ground every bet in evidence. If you get energy from designing for AI, using AI to accelerate your own ways of working, and operating with a high degree of autonomy, this role is for you. Own and scale a cross-cutting product domain. Define and continuously refine the multi-quarter and multi-year strategy for a product area that spans multiple teams and surfaces, ensuring it ladders up to company and portfolio strategy—not just a single feature set. Set direction through outcomes, not output. Establish clear, measurable product outcomes; translate them into coherent roadmaps; and make deliberate tradeoffs that balance short-term wins with long-term strategic bets. Drive complex cross-functional execution. Lead cross-team initiatives that require coordination across product, engineering, design, data/AI, GTM, customer-facing teams, and partners. Anticipate and remove systemic blockers, resolve conflicts, and ensure we ship high-quality, high-impact releases on schedule. Act as a thought partner to senior leadership. Synthesize customer, market, competitive, and internal data into crisp narratives and recommendations that influence portfolio and business strategy. Lead strategy documents, investment cases, and executive reviews for your domain. Be deeply data-driven at scale. Define and instrument success metrics for your domain, design experiments, and use quantitative and qualitative signals to make decisions, validate hypotheses, and iterate. Model best practices in how we use data to steer product direction. Design for AI and build AI-native experiences. Identify and prioritize where AI and agentic capabilities create disproportionate value for our industrial users. Partner closely with engineering and AI teams to shape use cases, UX, and guardrails. Use AI tools proactively in your own discovery, analysis, and execution. Lead through ambiguity. Take ownership of problem spaces where the market signal is incomplete, constraints are evolving, or the solution requires structured experimentation. Bring structure, frameworks, and focus to ambiguous domains and help others navigate them. Be the trusted voice of the customer and the field. Build deep relationships with strategic customers, partners, and field teams. Regularly engage in discovery, design partner programs, and high-stakes customer conversations. Use these insights to challenge assumptions and refine strategy. Raise the bar for product quality and UX. Champion simplicity, usability, and technical soundness. Collaborate tightly with design and engineering to ensure we ship experiences that are robust, scalable, and delightful for industrial users. Mentor and elevate other product managers. Coach PMs across the team on craft, frameworks, and decision-making. Share best practices, review their work, and help shape and continuously improve product management standards and rituals at Seeq. Proven Principal-level impact in B2B SaaS. 7+ years of product management experience (or equivalent), including substantial time operating at Senior or Principal PM level on complex products or platforms. Track record owning strategy for complex domains. Demonstrated experience defining and executing multi-quarter roadmaps for cross-cutting or platform capabilities (e.g., data, analytics, workflow, AI/ML, or developer/platform surfaces). Enterprise and ecosystem mindset. Experience building products for enterprise customers—ideally in data, analytics, industrial software, or adjacent domains—balancing user needs, technical constraints, security, and commercial

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - API
jobs.description: Product Jobs / Senior Product Manager - API Posted 4 days ago Tekmetric Senior Product Manager - API 5 day week Built In Best Places '26 Remote · USA Job Description About Tekmetric About Tekmetric Tekmetric is the all-in-one, cloud-based platform helping auto repair shops run smarter, grow faster, and serve customers better. Built by a shop owner and shaped by the needs of the industry, Tekmetric brings together innovation, real-world experience, and a thriving community to help shops thrive - not just survive. From running a shop, to securing payments to engaging customers, our platform simplifies operations so shop owners can focus on what really matters: delivering exceptional service, earning trust, and growing sustainably. Officially founded in Houston in 2017, Tekmetric has grown from a single shop’s vision to the industry’s leading solution - all by staying true to our values of transparency, integrity, innovation, and a service-first mindset. But we’re not just building software. We’re building a movement. We’re empowering repair shops to rise above the daily grind, create meaningful connections with their customers, and lead the industry forward - one interaction at a time. Come build with us. Join the journey. Shape the future of auto repair. Working the Tekmetric Way At Tekmetric, we’re building a culture where winning matters - not for ego, but because when our customers win, we win together. We move fast, stay curious, and take full ownership of our results — no excuses, no finger-pointing. If you thrive in ambiguity, take initiative, and view honest feedback as fuel for growth, you’ll feel right at home here. We’re direct but respectful, ambitious yet grounded, and collaborative at every level. Everyone leads through impact and is encouraged to speak up, share ideas, and challenge assumptions (even your manager’s). This is a place for builders, not bystanders. Success here takes focus, follow-through, and a willingness to roll up your sleeves — but if you’re driven by meaningful work and real results, it’s deeply rewarding. You’ll join a team that cares about the work, supports one another, and takes smart risks to achieve bold goals. Be yourself, stay mission-focused, and you’ll thrive. If that energizes you, we can’t wait to meet you. At Tekmetric, great work happens anywhere, but great teams are built through intentional connection . We offer hybrid and remote work models based on your proximity to our office hubs. Because we value in-person collaboration, travel is an expected part of every role. We come together several times a year for team and company-wide offsites to align on goals and strengthen relationships. Attendance at these events is expected and fully supported. What You’ll Do Tekmetric is seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the development and growth of our SaaS product for auto repair shops. As a part of a relatively small and growing team, you will have the unique opportunity to become influential in shaping our product. You will be responsible for guiding the product strategy and roadmap, as well as managing the development, launch, and ongoing optimization of the product. Key Responsibilities: Lead Strategic Product Initiatives : Drive the planning and execution of product initiatives from concept to launch, ensuring alignment with business goals and market trends. Become and expert in the Auto Repair Industry : Develop a deep understanding of the Auto Repair domain in order to best identify new product opportunities for our core users. Lead Key AI Product Initiatives: Combine a deep understanding of the problem space with generative AI solutions to help customers do their jobs better and faster. User-Centric Research : Deeply understand our core users’ JTBD, concept testing, and usability testing into an iterative development process. Collaborate Across Teams : Work with design, engineering, product marketing, and sales teams to align on product vision, strategy, go-to-market plans, pricing, packaging, and business priorities. Deliver High-Performing Products : Ensure products meet users’ unmet needs while mitigating business risks in value, feasibility, and usability. Measure Business Impac t: Build products with business outcomes in mind then measure if those outcomes showed up. Develop Product Vision : Define and communicate the long-term vision of the product area you own and translate that into executable roadmaps. Train Internal Teams : Educate sales, support, and consulting teams on new technology, ensuring they can sell, troubleshoot, and use it effectively. Own the API Product Strategy: Define and drive the vision, roadmap, and go-to-market strategy for Tekmetric's API as a standalone product — including developer experience, versioning, and partner enablement. Drive API Adoption and Growth : Treat the API as a growth lever; identify integration opportunities with third-party platforms and partners, and work closely with sales and partnerships to expand the

jobs.title: Automotive Product Management Intern (f/m/x)
jobs.location: Automotive Product Management Intern (f/m/x) Munich (Hybrid) Full-time Intern / Student
jobs.description: Skip to main content English English Back to all jobs Automotive Product Management Intern (f/m/x) Munich (Hybrid) Full-time Intern / Student Apply for this job Your mission: As our Automotive Product Management Intern you will play an integral role in establishing partnerships with leading automotive brands in the industry as well as other mobility service providers. You will actively contribute to the team success by developing our product portfolio and supporting our in-car integration. How you contribute to our success: Defining our products and shaping our use cases: You will contribute to our long-term success and growth by building up our product portfolio with a focus on our automotive partners including the complete life-cycle management. Enabling strategic decisions by means of in-depth market analysis: You will support our Mobility & Automotive Integration Team by addressing important questions for data-driven decisions around all activities and the project/product management in close cooperation with our automotive partner. Creating customer-oriented presentations of our product: You prepare and contribute presentations and pitches that explain products or services to existing and prospective customers, thus supporting all sales, marketing, and partnership activities with automakers and mobility service-providers. Capturing and communicating customer requirements to our Tech Team: You identify forward-looking as well as sought-after features on the market and help to integrate them into our development pipeline in cooperation with our software experts. Engineering and establishing internal processes and tools for 4screen and the Mobility & Automotive Integration Team: You get the opportunity to shape our company and teamwork mode by proactively bringing up and implementing new ideas and best practices to optimize the efficiency in our internal processes. Coordinating our car pool fleet: You take ownership of our fleet's day-to-day operations, including scheduling maintenance and driving vehicles to partner auto shops, ensuring our test and demo cars stay in operation. What makes you stand out: You are currently enrolled in a master’s program or near completion of a bachelor’s degree . Ideally in the field of Business Administration with a focus on Entrepreneurship, Strategy, Marketing (or similar), Business Engineering or Business Informatics. Full-time availability for a 6 months internship is required. A valid drivers license allowing you to operate cars in Germany is necessary. Proficiency in English (C1 or higher) and German (B2 or higher) is required. You have the ability to work independently and take ownership. You are curious to learn, ready to deep dive into new topics, and actively offer support by putting insights into action. You are a strong team player and have good communication skills . You have experience with MS Office . Previous internship or working student experience (ideally in consulting, tech, automotive industry, or similar) is a plus. Please include a current university transcript of records and, if available, reference letters (Arbeitszeugnisse) from previous employers with your application. What we offer you: Gain firsthand experience in a dynamic and professional startup setting. Participating actively in crucial Automotive Product Management processes and acquiring knowledge of best practices from top notch professionals. Opportunity to work closely with the biggest OEM brands . Relax during your 20 days of annual leave (+ public holidays). Take care of your well-being with the support of our Egym Wellpass membership . Enjoy our flexible working hours policy in our o pen office in Munich’s vibrant Schwabin g district. Have fun and enjoy the great team spirit at regular Team Events & Offsites , Game Nights, etc. We stand for equal opportunity for everyone and value diversity. Therefore, we encourage an open and inclusive work environment where you can be exactly who you are. We truly believe that individual strengths and personalities make a great contribution to our success story. Hence, even if you don’t fulfill all the points above but believe that you are a great addition to our team and 4screen is a place you’d love to work at, we can only encourage you to apply! About us 4screen is the world’s first Driver Interaction Platform , connecting brands & businesses with on-the-go customers in real time – directly through the car screen. By operating within the vehicle’s native digital interface, 4screen leverages dynamic car data to enhance the mobility experience – all in full compliance with the highest safety and privacy standards. 4screen is integrated into the operating systems of 16 leading automotive brands, including Mercedes-Benz, Audi, Volkswagen, Toyota, Kia, Hyundai, and Stellantis. Today, more than 100 brands – including McDonald’s, Shell, and Waitrose – across 12 countries are already using the platform to reach drivers in meaningful, high-intent moments. Our team at 4screen consists of a mix of young talents and industry experts that have previously been in leading positions at companies like BMW, Google or BCG. We are building a highly innovative company where everything is dynamic, disruptive, and exciting - and that you can be part of! Are you ready to join the ride and shape the future of mobility? Apply for this job Powered by Personio Data privacy statement Legal Notice

jobs.title: Team Lead, Product Operations (Abóboda Factory)
jobs.description:
Job Title

Team Lead, Product Operations (Abóboda Factory)

Liderar e coordenar as operações diárias da equipa de Product Operations, garantindo a eficiência dos processos, a correta alocação de recursos e a execução das prioridades definidas. Atuar como ponto de referência para a equipa, promovendo a melhoria contínua e a colaboração com stakeholders internos para otimizar resultados e suportar os objetivos do negócio.

Principais Responsabilidades

  • Coordenar e supervisionar as atividades diárias da equipa de produção.
  • Definir prioridades, distribuir tarefas e assegurar a utilização adequada dos recursos disponíveis.
  • Apoiar o desenvolvimento da equipa através de formação, orientação e acompanhamento contínuo.
  • Contribuir para avaliações de desempenho, planeamento de recursos e melhoria de processos.
  • Garantir o cumprimento de políticas, procedimentos e objetivos operacionais.
  • Identificar e implementar iniciativas de melhoria contínua para aumentar a eficiência e a qualidade das operações.
  • Colaborar com equipas multifuncionais para otimizar processos end-to-end e melhorar a entrega de produtos e serviços.
  • Monitorizar indicadores operacionais e apoiar a resolução de problemas e a tomada de decisões.

Requisitos

  • Experiência em ambiente fabril, indústrial ou áreas similares.
  • Experiência na coordenação de equipas ou liderança funcional de projetos.
  • Conhecimento de gestão e melhoria de processos.
  • Conhecimento em Lean, 5s e melhoria continua.
  • Experiência na análise de dados e acompanhamento de KPIs.
  • Liderança e capacidade de influência.

What we can offer you:       

  • 🌎 A truly global DNA - Everything at Amadeus is global, from our people to our business, which translates into our footprint, processes, and culture.
  • 🎓 Great opportunities to learn - Learning happens all the time and in many ways at Amadeus, through on-the-job training, formal learning activities, and day-to-day interactions with colleagues.
  • 🤗 A caring environment - Amadeus fosters a caring environment, nurturing both a fulfilling career and personal and family life. We care about our employees and strive to provide a supportive work environment.
  • 🌟 A flexible working model - We want our employees to do their best work, wherever and however it works best for them.
  • 🌈 A diverse and inclusive community - We are committed to leveraging our uniquely diverse population to drive innovation, creativity, and collaboration across our organization.
  • 📈 A Reliable Company - Trust and reliability are fundamental values that drive our actions and shape long-lasting relationships with our customers, partners, and employees.
  • 🎯 A critical mission and purpose - At Amadeus, you will be powering the future of travel and pursuing a critical mission and extraordinary purpose.

#LI-EMEA

Diversity & Inclusion

Amadeus aspires to be a leader in Diversity and Inclusion in the tech industry, enabling every employee to reach their full potential by fostering a culture of belonging and fair treatment, attracting the best talent from all backgrounds, and as a role model for an inclusive employee experience.  

Amadeus is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender, race, ethnicity, sexual orientation, age, beliefs, disability or any other characteristics protected by law.  

Be aware of recruitment scams


Amadeus Group never charges fees, requests payment, or asks for financial information during recruitment. All legitimate opportunities are communicated solely through official Amadeus channels, including our careers website. Any payment request or outreach via unofficial platforms (e.g., WhatsApp, Telegram) should be treated as fraudulent.

jobs.title: Product Manager Agentic AI Education (m/w/d)
jobs.description: Agentic AI ist deine Welt – und du willst dich damit beschäftigen, statt ständig etwas verkaufen zu müssen? Du hast dich intensiv mit KI-Agenten beschäftigt, eigene Workflows oder Anwendungen gebaut und kennst Themen wie n8n, Make, APIs, Tool Calling, RAG, Vektordatenbanken oder MCP aus der Praxis? Dann bau mit uns den besten Agentic-AI-Kurs im deutschsprachigen Raum. Aufgaben Der Titel heißt Product Manager – gemeint ist aber jemand, der Lerninhalte baut, nichtverwaltet: halb Instructional Designer, halb Agentic-AI-Bastler. Du gestaltest das Produkt „Kurs“, von der ersten Demo bis zum fertigen Modul. Dabei beschäftigst du dich mit uns zusammen unter anderem mit folgenden Fragen: Was müssen Menschen heute über Agentic AI wissen? Welche Tools, Modelle und Frameworks sind wirklich relevant? Welche Agenten und Automatisierungen sollten Teil des Kurses sein? Wie lassen sich technische Inhalte verständlich und praxisnah vermitteln? Welche Übungen, Projekte und Demos bringen Teilnehmer wirklich weiter? Du unterstützt uns dabei, Inhalte zu strukturieren, praktische Anwendungen zu bauen, Lernmaterialien zu entwickeln und Referenten fachlich zu begleiten. Einzelne Inhalte kannst du später auch selbst unterrichten. Qualifikation Wir suchen keinen Verkäufer und keinen klassischen Bildungsmanager – sondern jemanden mit technischem Hintergrund, der bereits tief in Agentic AI steckt, neue Entwicklungen aufmerksam verfolgt und komplexe Dinge verständlich erklären kann. Ob Berufseinsteiger mit starkem Portfolio oder mit ein paar Jahren Erfahrung in Content, Training oder Produktentwicklung – entscheidend ist, was du bereits gebaut oder vermittelt hast, nicht dein Titel. Benefits Kleines Team, kurze Wege Hybrides Arbeits-Setup: Büro in Berlin-Mitte + Homeoffice Tägliche Arbeit mit den aktuellsten KI-Tools und -Entwicklungen Echte Verantwortung ab Tag 1 - du verwaltest nicht, du baust auf Perspektive auf eine wachsende Rolle in einem wachsenden Bereich Vollzeit, unbefristet. Schick uns nicht nur deinen Lebenslauf – zeig uns, womit du dich beschäftigst und was du bereits gebaut oder entwickelt hast (z.B. Kurskonzepte, Workshops, Tutorials oder Prototypen)! jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - House Owner & Service Request Acquisition (all genders)
jobs.description: Responsibilities We are looking for an experienced, passionate, and hands-on Senior Product Manager to join our product team and grow the top of our marketplace funnel. Aroundhome is a two-sided marketplace platform where the possibility of shared success begins with the match: the moment a homeowner is connected with the right partner. The House Owner & Service Request Acquisition domain owns everything upstream of that match. It starts when a homeowner discovers Aroundhome through search or content, continues as they describe their home project, and ends when a qualified service request is handed to matching. The quality of that request shapes the experience on both sides of the marketplace, so acquisition volume, conversion, and data quality are all part of your responsibility. At Aroundhome, Product Managers play a pivotal role in shaping the success of Germany's leading housing project platform. We work in a domain-based model, where each product team owns a clearly defined part of the platform. This structure fosters close collaboration within cross-functional teams and ensures that the expertise for continuous product discovery, hypothesis development, and iterative improvement sits directly within each domain. As a Senior Product Manager, you drive and execute the strategy, set a clear direction, and make sure your domain delivers meaningful value to both customer groups, homeowners and partners, as well as to the business. You continuously shape domain strategy and vision, setting clear measurable goals and a roadmap for homeowner acquisition: steering the full acquisition mix across paid and unpaid channels with a clear focus on growing the organic share, improving conversion along the homeowner journey, and raising the quality of the service requests handed to matching. You actively rally those around you toward this vision. You grow sustainable, unpaid acquisition through SEO, AI search optimization and scalable acquisition systems that compound over time, while keeping paid channels an effective and efficient part of the mix. You design and read high-traffic conversion experiments to lift conversion and data quality. You are responsible for building a robust architecture and foundation that supports our acquisition experience. You ensure our setup is scalable and ready for automated qualification, enabling seamless processes that benefit the entire marketplace. You identify where AI genuinely improves the homeowner experience and the platform's qualification processes, and you bring those opportunities into production together with Engineering, from first prompt experiments to shipped, measurable features. You drive domain growth through continuous end-to-end product discovery, using qualitative insights (interviews, usability tests, feedback, NPS) and quantitative data to identify opportunities and make evidence-based, outcome-driven decisions with measurable impact. You communicate with clarity and purpose, translate complex system and data topics into compelling narratives, and foster strong collaboration across Product, Tech, Product Analytics, UX, SEO and Content, Sales, and cross-domain stakeholders. You help carry the shift from a sales-driven organization toward a product platform. You ensure a strong balance between long-term strategic goals and short-term trade-offs, driving continuous lean delivery through effective backlog management and close collaboration with Engineering and Design. Skills You have hands-on experience building organic acquisition and understand SEO fundamentals (topical authority, local SEO, structured data, page performance) as well as how AI-driven search and assistants are changing discovery (AEO), ideally within a marketplace or high-volume consumer platform. You are experienced in optimizing complex user funnels and conversion flows, using experimentation (A/B testing) and data to improve conversion, quality, and user trust while laying the foundation for long-term platform engagement. You apply strong analytical thinking and product analytics skills to define success measures, interpret data, uncover insights, and guide evidence-based decisions that improve homeowner and business outcomes. You bring a strong technical affinity to help shape our underlying acquisition and conversion architecture. You understand the concepts of data models or taxonomies and have enough grasp of APIs and data flows to reason about feasibility and sequencing with engineers. You work with LLMs as a natural part of your daily practice and can show concretely how they strengthen your discovery, analysis, and delivery. Ideally, you have taken AI beyond personal productivity and understand how scalable AI solutions reach production-grade features, for example through prompt chains, LLM-supported data extraction, or automated qualification flows. You bring order into chaos by bringing together diverse perspectives, challenges, and priorities to establish shared understanding and stro

jobs.title: Associate Product Manager, Delivery (m/f/d)
jobs.description: We are looking for a Associate Product Delivery Manager to help turn product priorities into clear, well-organized work for our engineering and design teams. Working closely with our Product Lead, you will help define new features, prepare upcoming work, coordinate requirements, and keep track of sprint progress. You will bring structure and clarity to the product-development process while growing your product-management skills through hands-on experience and mentorship. This is a good opportunity for someone early in their product career who is organized, curious, and comfortable collaborating across different disciplines. Tasks Draft feature scopes, requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria with guidance from the Product Lead. Help maintain and organize the product backlog. Prepare features and tasks for refinement and sprint planning. Coordinate requirements and open questions between product, engineering, and design. Track sprint status, delivery progress, dependencies, and blockers. Keep the team and relevant stakeholders informed about progress and changes. Support planning, refinement, reviews, and other product-delivery meetings. Document product requirements, decisions, and changes. Gather the information needed for prioritization discussions. Contribute recommendations while the Product Lead retains final prioritization responsibility. Help improve the team’s product-development and delivery processes. Take on greater product ownership as your skills and experience develop. Requirements What we’re looking for Approximately 1–3 years of relevant experience in product management, project coordination, business analysis, software delivery, design, QA, or a related area. Relevant internships and working-student experience may count. Some experience working with software engineering, design, or other multidisciplinary teams. The ability to turn discussions and ideas into clear written requirements and action items. Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. Clear and thoughtful written and verbal communication. Confidence following up on open questions, dependencies, and commitments. Curiosity about users, technology, and how digital products are built. A collaborative mindset and willingness to ask questions and learn. Basic familiarity with agile product development and tools such as Jira, Linear, Notion, or Confluence. Helpful, but not required Experience writing user stories or acceptance criteria. Familiarity with sprint planning, backlog refinement, or software-release processes. Enough technical curiosity to learn about APIs, data flows, system dependencies, and implementation trade-offs. Experience working in a startup, scale-up, or another quickly changing environment. You do not need to meet every item on this list. We care about your potential, judgment, communication skills, and willingness to learn. What success looks like within your first six months: Upcoming features and tasks are documented clearly and prepared on time. Sprint progress, dependencies, and blockers are visible to the team. Product decisions and requirement changes are consistently documented. Engineering and design know where to find the information they need. Open questions are identified and brought to the right people early. The Product Lead has reliable support across planning, prioritization, and delivery. You begin handling smaller features with increasing independence. Benefits We offer an exciting environment at the interface between IT and entertainment and marketing as part of an ambitious, international and results-orientated team in a young startup. You work with an excellent international team with varied tasks with a wide range of great opportunities An attractive and competitive salary Monthly learning budget Urban Sports Club membership support Support for your favourite means of transport - we help finance your new bike or your public transport subscription We are a startup in one of the cultural hubs in Berlin, you'll never get bored We welcome applicants with different backgrounds and experiences. If the role interests you but you do not meet every listed qualification, we still encourage you to apply. Find Jobs in Germany on Arbeitnow

jobs.title: Werkstudent:in Product Configuration (m/w/d)
jobs.description: Diese Aufgaben warten auf Dich ​• Du unterstützt uns dabei, unsere Produkte und internen Abläufe jeden Tag ein Stück besser zu machen und entwickelst ein gutes Verständnis dafür, was im Hintergrund sauber laufen muss. • Du testest unsere Produkte im Alltag, spürst Bugs und Fehler auf und dokumentierst sie so, dass das Team schnell damit weiterarbeiten kann. • Du bereitest Tickets und Workflows vor, prüfst, ob alle relevanten Informationen vollständig sind, und hilfst dabei, Prozesse sauber und effizient aufzusetzen. • Du erstellst und pflegst unsere API Dokumentation und trägst dazu bei, dass Wissen im Team gut nutzbar und verständlich festgehalten wird. • Je nach Deinem Kenntnisstand und Deiner Entwicklung übernimmst Du Schritt für Schritt mehr Verantwortung, zum Beispiel bei der eigenständigen Konfiguration von Produkten sowie bei der Erstellung dynamischer PDF Dokumente, E Mails und Pricing Algorithmen. Das bringst Du mit • Du befindest Dich mindestens im 2. Semester eines Bachelorstudiums, idealerweise mit technischem Bezug, zum Beispiel in Informatik, Maschinenbau oder Bauingenieurwesen. • Du bringst erste Kenntnisse in Programmierung mit und hast Lust, Dich weiterzuentwickeln, Neues zu lernen und Dich tiefer in technische Themen einzuarbeiten. • Du bist neugierig auf neue Technologien, insbesondere AI, und hast Lust, auszuprobieren, wie sie Deine Arbeit sinnvoll unterstützen können. • Du arbeitest sorgfältig, strukturiert und zuverlässig und bringst Aufgaben mit einem hohen Qualitätsanspruch zu Ende. • Du gehst proaktiv an Themen heran, bleibst offen im Austausch und lässt Dich nicht entmutigen, wenn etwas nicht auf Anhieb funktioniert. • Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch und Englisch. Das bieten wir Bei Alteos erlebst Du eine außergewöhnliche Kombination für die Versicherungsbranche. Hier treffen unternehmerische Freiheit, technisches Know-how und die Zuverlässigkeit unseres Investors AXA aufeinander. Gemeinsam gestalten wir innovative Lösungen und setzen neue Maßstäbe in der Versicherungsbranche. Werde Teil unseres Teams und arbeite mit talentierten und vielseitigen Kollegen zusammen. Für uns ist das tägliche Miteinander eine große Motivation – und wir hoffen, dass Du diese Begeisterung teilen wirst! Wir wachsen gemeinsam Gestalte die Zukunft von Alteos mit – bring dich in Projekte ein und beschreite neue Wege. Arbeitet an der Schnittstelle zwischen Versicherung und Technologie und entwickle dich zum gefragten Experten. Wir fördern eine Firmenkultur, die Diversität schätzt, Leistung honoriert sowie Vertrauen und Transparenz lebt. Bei uns findest du flache Hierarchien und kannst dich individuell entfalten. Wir bieten Dir einen unbefristeten Arbeitsvertrag und ein faires Gehalt. Alle 9-18 Monate gibt es eine Steigerung deines Gehalts als Anerkennung deiner Leistungen. Genieße vertrauensbasierte Arbeitszeiten und ein hybrides Modell aus Büro, Homeoffice und gelegentlichem Arbeiten aus dem europäischen Ausland. Du erhältst 27-30 Tage Jahresurlaub (je nach Betriebszugehörigkeit). Wir unterstützen Deine Weiterentwicklung mit Fortbildungen und bieten Dir zusätzlich Essenszuschüsse sowie Rabatte auf die VBB-Umweltkarte und Dienstrad-Leasing. Find Jobs in Germany on Arbeitnow

jobs.title: Working Student Product Configuration (m/w/d)
jobs.description: These tasks are waiting for you • You help us continuously improve our products and internal processes and develop a strong understanding of what needs to run smoothly behind the scenes. • You test our products in day to day use, identify bugs and issues, and document them in a way that helps the team move quickly. • You prepare tickets and workflows, check that all relevant information is complete, and help set up processes in a clean and efficient way. • You create and maintain our API documentation and help ensure that knowledge is documented clearly and can be easily used across the team. • Depending on your level of experience and development, you will gradually take on more responsibility, for example by configuring products independently and creating dynamic PDF documents, emails, and pricing algorithms. This is what you bring to the table • You are at least in the second semester of a Bachelor's degree, ideally in a technical field such as computer science, mechanical engineering, or civil engineering. • You have initial programming knowledge and are eager to keep developing, learn new things, and deepen your understanding of technical topics. • You are curious about new technologies, especially AI, and enjoy exploring how they can meaningfully support your work. • You work carefully, in a structured and reliable way, and complete tasks with a high standard of quality. • You approach topics proactively, stay open in collaboration, and do not get discouraged when something does not work right away. What we offer Together with our investor, AXA, we offer a combination of entrepreneurial freedom, leading technological know-how, reliability and extensive experience in the conception and management of insurance solutions that is rather unusual for the insurance industry. When you become part of our team, you can expect to work with incredibly versatile and talented colleagues. The opportunity to lead and grow an exciting team The opportunity to shape the future of insurance by contributing to the strategy and success of Alteos and further advancing one of the worlds leading core insurance systems Modern working in the middle of Berlin's City-West, with a wide range of perks and benefits. To name a few: subsidized lunch, discount on BVG ticket, company (e-)bike leasing, urban sports club, mental well-being app, and - if needed/wanted- Visa and relocation support as well as German classes Many opportunities to connect with your colleagues outside of daily work, e.g.through team events, weekly yoga classes, team runs and more A flexible work model (hybrid working role) Find more English Speaking Jobs in Germany on Arbeitnow

jobs.title: Senior Product Owner Conversational AI (gn)
jobs.description: Unser Kunde ist ein großer deutscher Dienstleistungskonzern mit einem sehr großen Endkundenstamm und hohem Serviceaufkommen am Telefon. Diesen Service entwickelt er mit KI und Automatisierung konsequent weiter: einfache Anliegen übernimmt zunehmend die Technologie, die Menschen konzentrieren sich auf das Komplexe. Wir suchen den Kopf, der diese KI-gestützte Contact-Center-Lösung von der Konzeption bis in den Betrieb aufbaut und den telefonischen Kundenservice damit auf die nächste Stufe hebt. ​ Deine Aufgaben Mit direkter Berichtslinie zur Bereichsleitung Kundenservice verantwortest Du die Lösung von der Konzeption bis in den Betrieb und schlägst die Brücke zwischen Fachbereich und IT. Das beinhaltet im Detail: Du konzipierst die Lösung und treibst die Automatisierung im telefonischen Service voran. Du übersetzt die Anforderungen in klare User Stories für Voice- und Chatbots und verantwortest den Backlog. Du steuerst die Umsetzung Ende zu Ende, von Projektzielen und Budget bis zur Führung eines interdisziplinären, agilen Teams. Du führst Telefonie, CRM und Bots gemeinsam mit der IT zu einer Plattform zusammen und gestaltest ihr Zusammenspiel. Du hast den Markt im Blick, entscheidest über den Einsatz neuer Technologien und verantwortest Qualität und Effizienz im Kundenkanal. ​ Dein Profil Du hast mehrjährige Erfahrung als Product Owner / Produktmanager an der Schnittstelle von Business und IT, idealerweise im Bereich Voice oder Conversational AI. Du hast Voice-, Chat- oder Contact-Center-Lösungen umgesetzt, idealerweise KI-Projekte. Du kennst den telefonischen Kundenservice aus der Praxis und hast dort Verantwortung getragen. Du arbeitest agil, User Stories sind für Dich selbstverständlich; Zertifizierungen wie Scrum, PRINCE2 oder PMP sind ein Plus. ​ Wir bieten Eine Plattform, die Du gestaltest, nicht ein bestehendes System, das Du verwaltest. Verantwortung im Aufbau, mit eigenem Team, eigenem Budget und direktem Draht zur Bereichsleitung Kundenservice. Eine exponierte Rolle, die Service, Produkt und KI verbindet und Dich für die nächste Führungsaufgabe aufstellt. Ein attraktives, der Verantwortung entsprechendes Vergütungspaket ​ Noch ein paar Worte zum Schluss Der Prozess läuft komplett vertraulich. Vorab bekommst Du von uns einen ehrlichen Einblick in Unternehmen, Aufgabe und Rahmenbedingungen, und erst nach Deiner ausdrücklichen Freigabe gehen Unterlagen an unseren Kunden. Wir freuen uns auf den Austausch. Bitte lade aber vorher Deinen CV hoch, damit wir den grundsätzlichen Match einschätzen können. Find more English Speaking Jobs in Germany on Arbeitnow

jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
jobs.description: At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable. As a Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d), you will be a part of the Commercial Intelligence Team within our Commercial Analytics unit. You will be responsible for strengthening our analytics capabilities and directly impacting the company's success. In this highly visible role, you will drive the development of analytical products and standards that enable data‑driven pricing, marketing, and revenue decisions across the organization. Working closely with Revenue Management and Commercial Excellence, and Marketing stakeholders, you will translate complex data into actionable insights and scalable, automated solutions that support profitable and sustainable growth. About the Role Proven experience building, maintaining, and optimizing reporting solutions in Power BI and Excel Ability to proactively identify new reporting needs and improve existing analytical processes with business stakeholders Hands-on, autonomous, and well‑organized working style suited for a highly visible, impact‑driven role Experience translating business questions into insights and clearly communicating findings to non‑technical audiences Solid documentation skills to ensure accuracy and transparency of data products and reports Strong analytical mindset with the ability to support revenue optimization processes, profitability, and sustainable growth Contribute to advancing the know-how and skill-set within the team through sharing knowledge and best practice About You 5+ years of professional experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst Strong SQL skills and hands‑on experience working with Snowflake. Python, Power Platform, Jenkins/GitLab, and VS Code experience is considered an advantage Solid experience in building analytical models and reports using Power BI (connected to Snowflake) Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including complex formulas and macros. Experience with AI tools will be considered an advantage Experience working in Agile team environments, ideally alongside other analysts Proven ability to communicate insights effectively to internal and external non‑technical stakeholders Fluent English (spoken and written) We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! What We Offer Travel perks : 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. Work from (M)Anywhere : Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. Hybrid work model : We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. Wellbeing support : Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. Learning & Development : Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. Mentoring Program : Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career Why Join Flix? At Flix, you'll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path. As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work. If you're ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you! jobs.title: Senior Product Analyst
jobs.description: Team description The Central Product Analytics team sits at the heart of how SumUp understands and improves the experience of millions of merchants using our products every day. This role owns the Engagement domain — covering merchant engagement, product activation, support responses, and navigation across the SuperApp and web dashboard — and is the primary analytics partner for the Engagement product team. It's a genuine opportunity to take full ownership of a domain, build the measurement foundations from the ground up, and shift the team from reactive reporting to proactive, insight-led decision-making. What you'll do Define event taxonomies and measurement plans, implement and QA tracking (GA4), and make sure analytics is built into new product features from day one Build and maintain scalable datasets in Snowflake — including funnels, customer behaviour models, and feature adoption metrics — and use them to generate insights that directly inform product decisions Design and evaluate A/B tests: define success metrics, assess statistical validity, and communicate results clearly to product and engineering stakeholders Create and maintain intuitive dashboards and reusable query templates in Tableau that help teams answer their own questions without always needing an analyst Partner with product managers to develop and map out KPI trees, identify measurement gaps, and improve the quality and accuracy of engagement tracking across the domain You'll be great for this role if… Proven experience as a Data or Product Analyst, with a strong track record of working with customer behaviour data and translating it into product decisions. Advanced proficiency in SQL, including writing, optimising, and validating queries, building data models, and participating in code reviews. Strong knowledge of data visualisation tools such as Tableau or Power BI, and experience with data warehousing platforms like Snowflake, Postgres, or Redshift. Comfortable designing and analysing end-to-end experiments, including A/B tests and feature flag rollouts, with a solid understanding of statistical validity. Ability to communicate complex data findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, with strong written and verbal English skills. Why you should join SumUp 🌎 Opportunity to work with SumUppers globally on large-scale fintech products used by millions of businesses worldwide, from our Berlin office. This involves an office-first setup 🌈 Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion: be part of a workplace that values and promotes diversity, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone's perspectives are respected and embraced 🚀 Enrolment onto our Virtual Stock Option programme: you will own a stake in SumUp's future success 📚 A dedicated annual L&D budget of €2000 for your individual development, which can be used to attend conferences and/or advance your career through further education 💶 A corporate pension scheme where we match up to 20% of your contributions 🏖 Generous time off: enjoy 28 days of paid leave plus public holidays and special leave days 🏋️ Numerous other benefits such as Urban Sports Club subsidy, Kita placement assistance, subsidised office lunches 🌴 Break4me: 1-month sabbatical after 3 years of service 🔗 Referral Bonus: earn additional rewards by referring talented individuals to join the SumUp team About SumUp Be empowered to do more that matters. At SumUp, we're on a mission to empower small businesses across the globe by providing simple and affordable tools that allow them to thrive. Today, over 4 million businesses in 37 markets rely on SumUp as their financial partner to manage payments, finance and customer relationships. Our commitment to small businesses is reflected in our diverse team of over 3,000 SumUppers from over 90 nationalities, united by global collaboration and an innovative mindset. Our core values lay the foundation for who we are and what we stand for, shaping our work culture and driving our success. We foster inclusivity and a continuous learning culture, providing a safe space for personal and professional growth. Our differences make us unique and strong as we strive to create an environment where everyone belongs and feels supported, no matter how they identify. SumUp is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer, actively seeking and embracing diversity in our workforce. We don't make hiring or employment decisions based on race, colour, religion or religious belief, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age or any other basis protected by applicable laws or prohibited by company policy. Our commitment extends beyond recruitment to creating a safe and respectful workplace where harassment of any form is strictly prohibited. Discover more about our culture and opportunities on our careers website , and follow our journey on LinkedIn , Instagram , and TikTok . Job Application Tip We recognise that

jobs.title: Praktikant*in Brand & Product Marketing (m/w/d)
jobs.description: Jobbeschreibung Das Betriebssystem für die erneuerbaren Energien zu entwickeln, um nahtlose und effiziente Workflows zu gewährleisten, das ist unsere Mission. Unsere Mission ist systemrelevant. Sie maximiert die Performance der Erneuerbaren und somit deren Wirtschaftlichkeit. Unser großes Ziel dabei: eine Welt, in der grüne Energie das Wachstum sichert – der Erfolg der globalen Energiewende. Als Praktikant*in im Brand & Product Marketing arbeitest du an der Schnittstelle von Produkt, Marketing und Sales. Du übersetzt komplexe Technologie in klare Botschaften, baust Content, der wirkt, und hilfst dabei, Amperecloud als führende Marke im Software-Markt für erneuerbare Energien zu etablieren. Hiring Manager: Recruiter: Vertragsart: Praktikum, Vollzeit (mindestens 4 Monate) Arbeitsort: Berlin #Deine Aufgaben Content, der wirkt: Du recherchierst Themen, schreibst Texte und entwickelst Storys für Website, Newsletter, Whitepaper und Case Studies – vom ersten Entwurf bis zur Veröffentlichung. Product Marketing aufbauen: Gemeinsam mit Product und Sales übersetzt du Features in Positionierung, Messaging und Inhalte, die unsere Marke stärken und Leads generieren. Social Media Ownership: Du planst, erstellst und veröffentlichst Posts auf LinkedIn und weiteren Kanälen und verantwortest Community Management und Monitoring. Content Creation: Du produzierst authentische Fotos und Videos von Team, Produkt und Kund*innen – vor Ort, schnell und kreativ. Events und Messen: Du unterstützt bei der Vorbereitung und Gestaltung unserer Messeauftritte. Analyse und Wachstum: Du misst Wirkung, leitest Learnings ab, testest neue Formate und bringst eigene Ideen ein. Du nutzt AI-Tools, um manuelle Arbeit zu reduzieren und schneller zu werden. #Das bieten wir dir Hohe Sichtbarkeit bei Produkt-, Go-to-Market- und Leadership-Themen. Ownership ab Tag eins. Raum zu wachsen – gestalte deine Rolle aktiv mit. Weiterentwicklung: Anspruchsvolle Aufgaben, steile Lernkurve, Bildungsurlaub. Impact: Gestalte die Zukunft der erneuerbaren Energien mit. Team & Kultur: Regelmäßige Offsites, inspirierende Events, hundefreundliches Büro. Mobilität: JobRad-Leasing sowie ein bis zu 100 % bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket. Gesundheit & Vorsorge: Betriebliche Altersvorsorge, Zuschüsse zur Kinderbetreuung, Wellpass/Urban Sports. Flexibilität: Arbeit aus unserem modernen Berliner Büro mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten. Stellenanforderungen #Das bringst du mit Bachelor oder Master (oder laufendes Studium) in Kommunikation, Wirtschaft, Ingenieurwissenschaften oder einem vergleichbaren Fach; idealerweise Top 20 % an einer Top-Universität/Business School oder klare Belege für Exzellenz (z. B. Auszeichnungen, Wettbewerbe, eigene Projekte, veröffentlichte Arbeiten). Erste Praktika oder Werkstudentenrollen in leistungsstarken Umfeldern – High-Growth-Startup/Scaleup, Agentur mit anspruchsvollen Kunden, Consulting oder vergleichbare Intensität. Echten Anspruch, Product und Content Marketing aufzubauen , nicht nur Aufgaben abzuarbeiten: Du willst Themen end-to-end verantworten und sichtbare Ergebnisse liefern. Starkes Schreiben auf Deutsch und Englisch – du machst aus technischer Komplexität klare, überzeugende Botschaften. Tool-Kompetenz: Adobe Creative Suite oder vergleichbare Design-Tools, SEO-Grundlagen, idealerweise HubSpot; sicherer Umgang mit AI-Tools. Teamorientiert, low ego, verlässliche Umsetzung – du erkennst, priorisierst und erledigst Aufgaben eigenständig. Interesse an Energie, Klima und B2B-Software . Neugierig geworden? Dann schicke uns noch heute deine Bewerbung – wir freuen uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen! Wir verpflichten uns, jede qualifizierte Person nach ihren Fähigkeiten zu beurteilen, unabhängig von Alter, Geschlechtsidentität, ethnischer Zugehörigkeit, sexueller Orientierung, Behinderungsstatus oder Religion. jobs.title: Technical Product Manager (m/w/d) AI & Data Analytics Mietwagen
jobs.description: AI-Features von der Idee bis zum Go-Live ownen: Du identifizierst selbst, wo AI entlang der Customer Journey den größten Impact hat, konzipierst die Lösung und steuerst die Umsetzung mit den IT-Teams – vom Prototyp über Testing bis zum Launch Prozesse identifizieren und automatisieren: Du analysierst kontinuierlich interne und partnerseitige Abläufe nach Optimierungspotenzial und treibst deren Automatisierung eigenverantwortlich voran. Unser helles und modernes Büro bietet Raum für Kreativität, Arbeiten im Freien und Spaß beim Kicker oder Tischtennis. Dank kostenlosem Deutschlandticket, bezuschusstem Fahrrad-/ eBike-Leasing und kostenlosen Fahrradstellplätzen kommst Du klimaneutral und günstig ins Büro Bei uns bist Du flexibel: Flexible Arbeitszeiten, mehr als 30 Tage Urlaub bei sehr guter Leistung und die Option auf zusätzlichen unbezahlten Urlaub, Sabbaticals und Workation im Ausland Persönlicher Austausch und Teamspirit bringen Dich voran: Wir pflegen unsere Office Kultur mit persönlichem Austausch, enger Zusammenarbeit und großartigem Teamzusammenhalt. Freitags besteht die Möglichkeit für „Home Office“ (mobiles Arbeiten). Wir sind weit mehr als nur Kollegen – überzeug Dich selbst von unserem CHECKito Spirit Wir schätzen Deinen Einsatz: Unbefristete Verträge und eine attraktive Vergütung mit jährlichen Prämien für besondere Erfolge. Gutscheine für zahlreiche CHECK24 Produkte, bezuschusste betriebliche Altersvorsorge und viele weitere Benefits (z.B. EGYM Wellpass, Rückenschule, kostenlose Sportangebote, Teambudgets für Events) gibt es obendrauf Deine Ideen sind uns wichtig und werden schnell umgesetzt: Eigenverantwortliches und agiles Arbeiten unter Einbringung eigener Ideen. Kurze Entscheidungswege und Releasezyklen sind für uns selbstverständlich Wir bringen Dich voran: Individuelle Förderung Deiner Karriere durch interne und externe Fortbildungen, regelmäßige Feedbackgespräche und persönliches Coaching sowie ein persönliches Budget für Fortbildungen. Durch unser starkes und nachhaltiges Wachstum bieten wir Perspektiven in der Fach- und Führungslaufbahn Wir statten Dich aus: Einen leistungsstarken Laptop, zwei zusätzliche Bildschirme oder auf Wunsch einen Curved Monitor bekommst Du direkt am ersten Arbeitstag. Du wünschst Dir individuelles Equipment wie eine ergonomische Maus, Noise-Cancelling-Kopfhörer oder einen höhenverstellbaren Schreibtisch? Kein Problem! Bei uns kannst Du Dich wohlfühlen: Kostenlose Getränke gibt es gekühlt in jedem Büro. Kaffee, Kakao, Müsli und frisches Obst sind regional und bio. Kostenloses Mittagessen gibt es 5x pro Woche für das gesamte Team Wir sind auch neben der Arbeit ein super Team: Bei uns herrscht ein kollegiales und freundschaftliches Miteinander. Bei Biergarten- und Plärrerbesuchen, Office Partys und legendären Sommer- und Weihnachtsfeiern feiern wir gemeinsam unsere Erfolge. Zudem bieten wir zahlreiche Teamevents und gemeinsame Sportrunden

jobs.description: Skip to main content English English Back to all jobs Senior Product Owner - Digital Ops (all genders) Köln Full-time Permanent employee Apply for this job Job overview We’re looking for a Senior Product Owner (all genders) for the OPS Heartbeat Program — a strategic initiative designed to transform operational excellence. jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable.  

As a Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d), you will be a part of the Commercial Intelligence Team within our Commercial Analytics unit. You will be responsible for strengthening our analytics capabilities and directly impacting the company’s success. In this highly visible role, you will drive the development of analytical products and standards that enable data‑driven pricing, marketing, and revenue decisions across the organization. Working closely with Revenue Management and Commercial Excellence, and Marketing stakeholders, you will translate complex data into actionable insights and scalable, automated solutions that support profitable and sustainable growth.

About the Role

  • Proven experience building, maintaining, and optimizing reporting solutions in Power BI and Excel
  • Ability to proactively identify new reporting needs and improve existing analytical processes with business stakeholders
  • Hands-on, autonomous, and well‑organized working style suited for a highly visible, impact‑driven role
  • Experience translating business questions into insights and clearly communicating findings to non‑technical audiences
  • Solid documentation skills to ensure accuracy and transparency of data products and reports
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to support revenue optimization processes, profitability, and sustainable growth
  • Contribute to advancing the know-how and skill-set within the team through sharing knowledge and best practice

About You

  • 5+ years of professional experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst
  • Strong SQL skills and hands‑on experience working with Snowflake. Python, Power Platform, Jenkins/GitLab, and VS Code experience is considered an advantage
  • Solid experience in building analytical models and reports using Power BI (connected to Snowflake)
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills, including complex formulas and macros. Experience with AI tools will be considered an advantage 
  • Experience working in Agile team environments, ideally alongside other analysts
  • Proven ability to communicate insights effectively to internal and external non‑technical stakeholders
  • Fluent English (spoken and written)

We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you!  

What We Offer

  • Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
  • Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
  • Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
  • Wellbeing support: Access confiden

jobs.title: Group Product Manager, B2C
jobs.description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours. 

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Your mission

GetYourGuide's On-Trip product group owns the entire post-booking experience: from the moment a traveler completes checkout through fulfillment, supplier coordination, to agentic care resolution.   • Manage three PMs in Zurich. You report to the Director of Product, Traveler, who is based in Berlin. 

Your toolkit

  • Demonstrated ability to synthesise a complex, multi-pillar strategy into a clear and opinionated thesis for executive audiences with a coherent point of view beyond executional updates.

Growth Data Products (GDP) drives GetYourGuide's growth engine by transforming raw marketing data into reliable, self-service insights and automated decision systems. Our work spans every growth channel—Paid and Organic Search, Display Advertising, CRM and Lifecycle Marketing, Brand Marketing, Marketing Attribution, Experimentation, and Customer Lifetime Value measurement. jobs.title: Senior Director of Engineering, Developer Productivity
jobs.location: Alberta; British Columbia; Manitoba; Nova Scotia; Ontario; Quebec
jobs.description:
MongoDB’s Developer Productivity organization exists to help engineers build and deliver high-quality software through a highly effective software development process and a strong foundation of shared tools and services.

We are looking for a Senior Director to lead our Pipeline team. This role is tasked with bringing together the major systems and experiences that power software delivery at MongoDB. The team’s mission is to provide a reliable, scalable, secure, and effective platform for ensuring fast software deployability, leveraging AI native approaches.

We are open to in-office, flexible or remote hiring across Canada.

The Team

The Pipeline organization sits within Developer Productivity and is responsible for the systems, services, and user experiences that define MongoDB’s software delivery ecosystem.   This is mission-critical infrastructure operating at substantial scale and supports a variety of software product delivery needs. Success in this role requires excellent product judgment for developer-facing experiences, strong systems and platform leadership, and the ability to align multiple teams around a cohesive strategy.

Candidate Profile

We’re looking for a senior engineering leader who can unify product-minded developer tooling with deep platform and operational excellence. The right candidate is passionate about developer productivity and has a track record of leading managers and teams through organizational growth, technical complexity, and cross-functional change.

They should be comfortable owning a broad portfolio that spans developer experience, reliability and scale, release systems, telemetry, and operational health. They should also be able to work effectively with senior leaders and partners across engineering and product to set direction, allocate resources, and make trade-offs that balance near-term delivery with long-term platform function.

The right candidate for this role will

  • 12+ years of hands-on software engineering experience building and operating production systems; experience in developer tooling, platform engineering, observability, or data engineering is a strong plus.
  • 6+ years of experience managing multiple teams and building a strong bench of leaders in a geographically distributed organization with a track-record of success in hiring, performance management, and fostering a team growth mindset.
  • Have a track record of scaling large software systems used by software engineers, ideally including CI/CD, release, or other developer infrastructure platforms with large scale workloads.
  • Bring strong judgment across both developer-facing product experience and underlying platform architecture, including user workflows, system reliability, operational insight, and service scalability.
  • Have experience with cloud infrastructure and infrastructure-as-code principles; experience with Kubernetes and AWS is especially valuable.
  • Understand how to use telemetry, metrics, and analytics to improve developer productivity and operational health.
  • Have experience or strong familiarity with software supply chain and release concerns, including reliability, security, and operational rigor.
  • Be able to define strategy and drive execution in ambiguous environments with broad organizational impact, acting as a trusted escalation point for complex technical and organizational trade-offs.

What you will do

  • Own the strategy, execution, and operating model for MongoDB’s end-to-end software delivery pipeline across continuous integration and release enablement functions.
  • Set portfolio priorities and resource allocation in partnership with leadership and cross-functional stakeholders, balancing urgent delivery needs with long-term platform investments.
  • Improve the experience of internal engineering users while also raising the bar on reliability, scalability, security, and operational efficiency.
  • Establish and evolve mechanisms for measuring developer productivity, system health, and business impact across the pipeline portfolio.
  • Build a healthy, high-performing organization by growing managers, strengthening succession depth, and creating clarity around expectations, accountability, and career development.
  • Serve as a senior escalation point for critical trade-offs and cross-functional issues, ensuring decisions are aligned to company priorities and understood in terms of their downstream consequences.

Positive expectations

  • Leads a large, strategic organization with a positive and inclusive culture while setting a high bar for accountability and execution.
  • Demonstrates mastery of both verbal and written communication to navigate a global organizational structure that utilizes a mix of synchronous and asynchronous engagement.
  • Collaborates eff

jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager, Developer Productivity
jobs.location: Alberta; British Columbia; Manitoba; Nova Scotia; Ontario; Quebec
jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Senior Engineering Manager who is ready to lead through ambiguity and improve how software gets built at MongoDB. This role leads teams focused on developer productivity, with an emphasis on measurable improvements to the software development lifecycle.

This role can be based remotely in Canada.

The Team

The AXIS team (AI, X-functional tools, Insights, and Signals) sits within Developer Productivity and is responsible for overseeing the metrics and observability infrastructure of our expansive developer environment to help build a strong data-driven culture.  You’ll also be a key partner in building the agentic ecosystem for AI-driven development across engineering.

Candidate Profile

We’re looking for an experienced leader with a passion for solving the big challenge of measuring developer productivity and providing the actionable signals that help teams improve their performance. They should be comfortable working collaboratively with other leaders and partners across our Engineering and Data teams in maximizing the use of data for insights and AI enablement. 

The right candidate for this role will have

  • 4+ years of experience managing software engineers, including hiring, performance management, growth planning, and compensation; required for external candidates and preferred for internal candidates
  • 8+ years of hands-on software engineering experience building and operating production systems; experience in developer tooling, platform engineering, observability, or data engineering is a strong plus
  • Demonstrated the ability to lead through ambiguity, work across team boundaries, and deliver outcomes without close supervision
  • Strong customer orientation and sound judgment in finding practical, high-leverage solutions
  • Experience working with systems involving analytics, data pipelines, and metrics platforms
  • Experience with AI tools development and enablement efforts
  • Strong technical judgment, including the ability to evaluate tradeoffs, identify risks, and ask the right questions without needing to be hands-on in the code every day
  • Ability to connect observability, engineering metrics, and AI capabilities to meaningful improvements in the developer ecosystem
  • Excellent communication skills with engineers, peer managers, and senior leadership
  • A track record of building inclusive, high-performing teams where engineers can do their best work

Position Expectations

  • Provide technical leadership and mentorship to a team of engineers, fostering a culture of innovation, quality, collaboration, and continuous improvement
  • Improve engineering visibility and decision-making. Ensure your team delivers the systems, analytics, and reporting needed for Engineering leadership to understand the health, performance, and efficiency of the software development lifecycle
  • Evaluate, integrate, and build AI-enabled interfaces and tools that drive engineer productivity improvements, with a focus on measurable impact and responsible rollout
  • Drive the roadmap. Partner with Staff and Senior Staff ICs, TPMs, and stakeholders to define and evolve a 12-month roadmap that balances near-term delivery with long-term platform investment

Success Measures

  • In the first month, understand the team structure, active projects, and stakeholder landscape. Build trust with your direct reports and identify the top execution risks
  • In three months, deliver meaningful improvements in at least one area, measured through adoption, reliability, or developer satisfaction, and establish yourself as a reliable partner to peers and leadership
  • In twelve months, complete a full performance and growth cycle for your team, define a coherent multi-domain strategy, and drive measurable improvements in engineering effectiveness and developer experience across MongoDB

 

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platform for the AI era, enabling builders to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified data platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platform on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud data platform and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

With offices worldwide and over 67,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.

Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership
